data.title: Schlumberger Non-GAAP EPS of $0.78 beats by $0.04, revenue of $9.75B beats by $200M
data.description:
• Schlumberger press release (SLB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.78 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $9.75B (+5.1% Y/Y) beats by $200M.
  • Fourth-quarter revenue of $9.75 billion increased 9% sequentially with international revenue increasing 8% and North America revenue increasing 15%.

data.title: Ryan Cohen keeps adding to his stake in GameStop
data.description:
GameStop Corp. (GME) CEO Ryan Cohen was active again in snapping up shares of the videogame retailer. Per an SEC filing, Cohen bought another 500,000 shares of GME during the week at a weighted average price of $21.60 per share. Cohen holds an aggregate position in GameStop (GME) of 42,082,626 shares, including warrants. Cohen has picked up more than 1.5M shares over the last year to take his total position in GME to about 9.3%.

Interestingly, it was five years ago this month that GameStop (GME) became the epicenter of the financial world with a stunning rally led by retail investors that flipped the script on some notable hedge funds. What started as an underdog bet on a video game retailer turned into a cultural phenomenon with a story so dramatic it ended up on the big screen.

Shares of GameStop (GME) were down 0.6% in postmarket trading to $23.01. The stock is up more than 15% on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on GME stands at 14.7% of the total float.

data.title: U.S. stock futures mixed amid caution after Intel earnings; Dow down 0.1%
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Friday, with S&P 500 futures slightly higher and Dow futures down 0.1%, as investors weighed global developments and central bank signals, capping a volatile week marked by disappointing earnings from Intel (INTC).

Futures tied to the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.03%, while Dow Jones Industrial Average (INDU) and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell 0.14% and 0.03%, respectively.

The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) eased to 4.24%, down 1 basis point, while the 30-year yield (US30Y) ticked down to 4.83%, shedding 12 basis points.

President Donald Trump on Thursday said the U.S. would essentially have “total access” to Greenland under the deal that is being negotiated with NATO, according to an interview with Fox Business.

News of a framework deal surfaced as Trump backed off tariff threats against Europe and ruled out using force to take Greenland, easing what had threatened to become the most serious rupture in transatlantic relations in decades.

The dollar index (DXY) was set for its biggest weekly drop in seven months, while emerging-market stocks and currencies continued a strong start to 2026.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR), meanwhile, briefly climbed above $4,950 an ounce before giving back gains, yet remained on track for its best week in nearly six years.

On the corporate front, Intel (INTC) dropped 13% in premarket trade after a lackluster forecast. Ericsson AB (ERIC) shares advanced as much as 12% after the telecom equipment maker proposed its first-ever buyback and reported a fourth-quarter earnings beat.

data.title: Openmarkets enters business combination with Lake Superior Acquisition
data.description:
• Openmarkets entered a business combination agreement with Lake Superior Acquisition (LKSP) (LKSPU), valuing Openmarkets at an estimated enterprise value of $300M and aims to list the combined entity on Nasdaq.
  • The transaction is expected to be completed in 2026.
  • "The combination with Openmarkets is the culmination of an exhaustive search for a market leader poised for explosive global growth. Through this transaction, we are providing the public market platform necessary for them to accelerate their expansion into new geographies and further their ambition to build the future of open finance through DeFi. said Edward Cong Wang, CEO, and Chairman of Lake Superior Acquisition.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares Non-GAAP EPS of $51.27 beats by $7.48, revenue of $2.44B beats by $210M
data.description:
• First Citizens BancShares press release (FCNCA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $51.27 beats by $7.48.
  • Revenue of $2.44B (+1.2% Y/Y) beats by $210M.
  • Net interest margin ("NIM") was 3.20% compared to 3.26% in the linked quarter, a decrease of 6 basis points. NIM, excluding PAA, was 3.11%, compared to 3.15% in the linked quarter, a decrease of 4 basis points.

data.title: China widens review of Meta’s acquisition of Manus: report
data.description:
China has deepened a probe into Meta Platforms' (META) $2B acquisition of Chinese-founded AI startup Manus, Bloomberg News reported.

Earlier this month, the Financial Times reported that China's commerce ministry was reviewing the acquisition for potential violations of technology export controls. The early-stage assessment examined if Manus's staff and tech relocation to Singapore required a Chinese export license before the sale.

The core issue is if Chinese technology or user data could have been compromised or shared with a U.S. company. Regulators are now also probing potential violations of rules governing cross-border currency flows, tax accounting, and overseas investments, the report added, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Manus’s co-founders and parent company, Butterfly Effect, began operations in China before moving its headquarters and staff to Singapore.

The probe into Meta’s acquisition is preliminary because it closed just weeks ago, and China could ultimately decide not to take any action, the report noted.

Some officials had privately shown support for Manus after the deal was announced. In addition, Manus’s backers have already received their money, making it difficult to unwind the transaction, the report added.

However, the deal has raised concerns in China as it marked a major U.S. company acquiring a well-regarded startup of Chinese origin that has been compared with the likes of OpenAI and China's own DeepSeek. Its rise during that time attracted early U.S. backers, including venture investor Benchmark.

Manus started to relocate its staff to Singapore from Beijing and Wuhan in July 2025, parting ways with dozens of staff unwilling to move at the time. The move caught the attention of Chinese regulators, which privately raised concerns about cross-border data transfers and taxes, the report noted.

Manus has focused on international markets almost from the beginning, and its main agentic AI product has never been available in China. However, one of Butterfly Effect’s previous products — Monica, a Chrome browser extension — is available. Regulators did not start an investigation earlier on the presumption that the company would maintain close ties with China, the report added.

Manus is known for its AI agents that help execute tasks for users, such as screening resumes, creating trip itineraries, and analyzing stocks in response to basic instructions.

data.title: Trump says U.S. negotiating 'total access' to Greenland - report
data.description:
President Donald Trump said the U.S. would essentially have "total access" to Greenland under the deal that is being negotiated with NATO, according to an interview with Fox Business.

"It's really being negotiated now, the details of it, but essentially it's total access," Trump said when asked about the Greenland deal. "There's no end, there's no time limit."

Trump previously said the U.S. and NATO formed a "framework of a future deal" with respect to Greenland and the entire Arctic Region, but did not provide any details. 

NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte and Trump agreed on further talks between the U.S., Denmark and Greenland on updating a 1951 deal that governs U.S. military presence on the island, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters. They also discussed potentially barring Chinese and Russian investments in Greenland. 

But another source said the talks between Rutte and Trump only reached "a frame on which to build" and "anything being reported on specific details is speculative."

To note, Rutte said mineral exploitation was not discussed at the meeting.

"I don't know what there is in the agreement, or the deal, about my country," said Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen. "We are ready to negotiate a better partnership and so on. But sovereignty is a red line."

Meanwhile, Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen called for a permanent NATO presence in the Arctic region, including around Greenland.

data.title: Volatility: Natural gas futures fall following record rally on Winter Storm Fern
data.description:
It's been several action-packed sessions for natural gas prices, which were on track to log their largest weekly gain in over three decades. Front-month Nymex futures (NG1:COM) surged some 60% since Monday to reach $5.656/MMBtu, but volatility has since resumed overnight. Futures have slipped back into the $4.00 range, with many an experienced trader knowing things can continue in the wild world of commodity trading.

What happened? The coldest and snowiest weather in years is about to set in nationwide, from New Mexico and Texas to New England. New York City is even bracing for temperatures in the single digits, with subzero temperatures coming to Minneapolis, Chicago and Detroit. The Arctic blast will likely impact around 230M Americans across as many as 30 states, triggering significant demand for household heating and the potential for shortages or even outages.

"While initially attributed to fears of European supply disruptions amid recent EU-US trade tensions, the reality is more complex," OANDA Group writes in a new SA article. "U.S. output sits at decade lows [as] persistently low prices disincentivized production following the record output of 2023-2024. Simultaneously, power generation demand is surging. The need to power AI data centers and metal smelting operations is outpacing futures delivery schedules, fueling this price acceleration. Stress on the system is [also] amplified by the US's role as the world's leading LNG exporter, particularly to Europe following the closure of Russian supply routes."

On watch: Popular natural gas funds like the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) and the ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Natural Gas ETF (BOIL) are up 22% and 10% for the week, respectively. Generator stocks have also taken off, with both Generac (GNRC) and Pioneer Power Solutions (PPSI) advancing 9% over the same timeframe. Meanwhile, major airlines have said they may face disruptions, with Delta (DAL), United (UAL), and JetBlue (JBLU) announcing they will waive change fees on certain flights impacted by Winter Storm Fern.

data.title: Ericsson shares jump on Q4 beat, dividend, and buyback plan; CEO signals further job cuts
data.description:
Ericsson’s (ERIC) U.S.-listed shares jumped 7% on Friday after the company reported fourth-quarter results that beat expectations and issued guidance, while announcing plans to propose a SEK 3.00 per share dividend for 2025 and a SEK 15 billion share buyback program.

Additionally, the Swedish telecoms equipment maker expects to continue cutting jobs, CEO Börje Ekholm said.

"You have seen that we have reduced the headcount, for example, by 5,000 over the past year, and we expect to continue reducing headcount going forward," Ekholm said in a post-earnings call.

Earlier in January, Ericsson (ERIC) said it would cut up to 1,600 jobs in Sweden.

The company reported organic sales growth in all three segments in Q4, with 12% growth in the segment Cloud Software and Services.

"For 2026, we expect the RAN market to be flat. Mission critical and enterprise markets, where we are well positioned, are expected to grow. In this environment, we plan to increase investments in defense during 2026 while continuing to optimize our cost base to support margins and cash flow generation,” Ekholm said.

The company reported a 6% year-on-year rise in sales, with growth in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, as well as South East Asia, Oceania and India, while the Americas were broadly stable and North East Asia saw a decline.”

data.title: China approves GSK’s Trelegy Ellipta for adult asthma treatment
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) said on Friday that China’s National Medical Products Administration approved a new indication for Trelegy Ellipta to treat adults with asthma, expanding its approved use beyond chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
  • The approval means that FF/UMEC/VI is the first and only single-inhaler triple therapy approved for the maintenance treatment of both respiratory conditions in the country.
  • Approval was based on GSK’s CAPTAIN study, which showed that in patients uncontrolled on inhaled corticosteroids/long-acting beta agonist (ICS/LABA), the additional bronchodilation provided by FF/UMEC/VI demonstrated significant improvements in lung function compared with FF/VI. 
  • Source: Press release
